rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to define what counts as a hedonic product versus a utilitarian one, and this definition is reused in the experimental design and product pretests. The citation is not just a background list item; it supports a core construct operationalization that the hypotheses and results depend on. At the same time, the citing paper does not test or revise Dhar and Wertenbroch's forfeiture-versus-acquisition mechanism directly.
relation_description: Ramanathan and Menon apply Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-versus-utilitarian goods distinction as a conceptual and measurement basis for classifying stimuli in their impulsivity studies. The cited work is used to transfer an established product-typing framework into a new context of time-varying impulsive behavior.
